title: Listening - Part 4: Rural Relocation
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about moving from a city to the countryside. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’d lived in London my whole life, but [21]the noise levels just got to a point where I couldn't sleep properly anymore[/21]. I was constantly on edge. Moving to a small village in Wales was a shock. What I love now, though, is [26]the feeling of safety for my kids[/26]. They can ride their bikes to the local shop without me hovering over them every second. That peace of mind is something you just can't buy in a metropolis. || Speaker 2: We moved because [22]my partner was offered a fantastic teaching position at a rural college[/22]. It wasn't really my plan, and I was worried about my own career. Surprisingly, [27]it’s actually been a huge boost for my physical health[/27]. I’m out hiking or gardening every day now, whereas in the city, the most exercise I got was walking to the tube station. I feel ten years younger. || Speaker 3: I’m a writer, and [23]I moved to the coast because I needed a place where I wouldn't be constantly tempted by social distractions[/23]. In the city, there was always a gallery opening or a dinner to go to. Here, it’s just me and the sea. The unexpected bonus has been [28]the much lower cost of living[/28]. My mortgage is half what it was, and I’m actually able to put money aside for retirement for the first time in my life. || Speaker 4: I’ve always been passionate about the environment, so [24]moving to a cottage was part of a larger project to become completely self-sufficient[/24]. We grow our own veg and have solar panels. It’s hard work, but [29]I’ve discovered a real talent for DIY and home maintenance[/29]. I used to call a plumber for a leaky tap, but now I’m doing everything myself, from tiling to basic electrics. It’s very empowering. || Speaker 5: I’m a tech consultant, and I moved [25]primarily because the air quality in the city was starting to affect my breathing[/25]. I needed clean air. It’s been great for my lungs. But the thing that’s really struck me is [30]the friendliness of the people here[/30]. In my old apartment block, I didn't know a soul. Here, the postman knows my name, and the neighbours actually stop for a chat. I feel like I’m part of a real community at last.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) the main reason each speaker gives for moving to the countryside.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 says the 'noise levels' meant they couldn't sleep and were on edge.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 moved because their 'partner was offered a fantastic teaching position'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted a place without 'social distractions' to focus on writing.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 moved to 'become completely self-sufficient' and grow food.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 moved because 'air quality' was affecting their breathing.

options.C: to be closer to extended family
options.E: to start a new business venture
options.F: to support a family member's career
options.G: to pursue a more sustainable lifestyle

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has found most rewarding about the move.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 values the 'feeling of safety for my kids' and peace of mind.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 says it's been a 'huge boost for my physical health'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 appreciates the 'much lower cost of living' and cheaper mortgage.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 discovered a 'real talent for DIY' and feels empowered.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 is struck by the 'friendliness of the people' and the community.

options.H: the warmth of the local residents
